human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for CPG trade-promotion specificity, source quality, lot and hold boundary handling, retailer evidence expectations, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to CPG trade-promotion controls, promotion readiness, retailer evidence packaging, lot-aware inventory checks, and blocked-event escalation.
Authority sources cover FTC promotion-law boundaries, FDA food-safety and traceability requirements, FDA recall handling, and GS1 identifier and item-data standards.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope execution, lot and inventory conflicts, quality holds, recurring incidents, supplier substitution pressure, low-confidence handling, conflicting-policy escalation, and tenant-specific adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/consumer-packaged-goods/trade-promotion-operations-specialist/ so trade-promotion operations remain distinct from adjacent quality, finance, procurement, and logistics lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around legal interpretation, financial approval, final release authority, recall handling, and supplier substitution approval.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated CPG trade-promotion operations specialist for promotion readiness, blocked-release routing, retailer evidence packaging, and corrective-action escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
